steps.prompt: Which word is the finite verb?

hints.Gaudium: 'Gaudium' is a noun meaning 'joy' — the subject
hints.caelo: 'Caelo' is a noun meaning 'heaven' in the ablative
hints.peccatore: 'Peccatore' is a noun meaning 'sinner' in the ablative

hints.present indicative: Present would be 'est' — 'erit' has the future -ri- formation
hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'sit' — 'erit' is future indicative

hints.eo, ire, ii, itum: These are the parts of 'ire' (to go)
hints.edo, esse, edi, esum: These are the parts of 'edere' (to eat) — the infinitive looks similar
hints.erro, errare, erravi, erratum: These are the parts of 'errare' (to wander/err)

hints.accusative: 'Gaudium' can be accusative, but here it is the subject of 'erit' — nominative
hints.vocative: One does not address 'joy' — this is the nominative subject

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'gaudii' ends in -i(i)
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'gaudii' ends in -i(i)
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'gaudii' ends in -i(i)

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'caelum' — 'caelo' follows 'in' indicating place
hints.dative: 'Caelo' could be dative, but after 'in' (place where) it is ablative

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'peccator' — 'peccatore' with -e is ablative
hints.dative: Dative would be 'peccatori' — 'peccatore' is ablative after 'super'

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'peccatoris' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'peccatoris' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'peccatoris' ends in -is

translation: There will be joy in heaven over one sinner
